user:
<user_query>
CONTEXTO
Estás a trabalhar NO REPO EXISTENTE da TecStock (já funcional). NÃO criar um produto novo.
O que eu quero é APENAS uma NOVA ABA (novo item no menu/nav) dentro da app existente, integrada com:
- autenticação/permissões atuais
- multi-tenant (restaurant/company/branch) como já existe
- UI/estilo/padrões da TecStock
- modelos/tabelas existentes (reutilizar sempre que possível)

OBJETIVO DA NOVA ABA
Criar uma nova aba no menu chamada: “Encomendas” (ou “Pedidos” se fizer mais sentido com a UX atual).
Nesta aba, o utilizador consegue:
1) Escolher um fornecedor
2) Escolher ingredientes/produtos existentes do sistema
3) Definir quantidade + unidade + notas por linha
4) O sistema gera automaticamente um EMAIL (subject + body) em pt-PT
5) O utilizador clica “Enviar pelo meu email” e abre o cliente de email via `mailto:` (GRÁTIS, sem Resend/SMTP/providers)
6) Guardar histórico destas encomendas (com snapshot do email gerado)

REQUISITO CRÍTICO
- O envio é via `mailto:` para funcionar em desktop e MOBILE (iOS/Android) abrindo o mail app do utilizador.
- Não usar Resend, SMTP, SendGrid, SES, nem qualquer provider. O backend NÃO envia emails.
- Isto tem de funcionar bem em mobile: layout responsivo, botões grandes, sem modais pesados que quebrem em ecrãs pequenos.

FASE 0 — ANALISAR O REPO (OBRIGATÓRIO)
Antes de codar:
- localizar onde adicionar um item novo ao menu/nav
- identificar como funciona o scoping por tenant (restaurant/company/branch) e permissões
- identificar entidades já existentes: Fornecedor, Produto/Ingrediente, Unidades, Stock movements, Compras (se existir)
- identificar padrão de rotas (App Router/Pages Router/Vue Router/etc), padrão de forms, validação, tabelas, e components reutilizáveis

INTEGRAÇÃO DE DADOS (NÃO DUPLICAR)
Reutilizar entidades existentes de:
- fornecedores (com campo email)
- ingredientes/produtos
- unidades (se existir)
Se não existir uma entidade de “encomenda ao fornecedor”, criar o mínimo necessário com migrations no padrão do projeto.

MODELO DE DADOS (CRIAR APENAS SE NÃO EXISTIR)
Criar tabelas/entidades mínimas (com scoping por tenant):
- SupplierOrder (ou nome consistente com o projeto)
  - id
  - tenantId/restaurantId (como o projeto usa)
  - supplierId
  - status: DRAFT | READY_TO_SEND | OPENED_MAIL_CLIENT | CANCELLED (NÃO criar SENT porque mailto não confirma envio)
  - desiredDeliveryDate (opcional)
  - emailSubjectSnapshot
  - emailBodySnapshot
  - createdBy
  - createdAt/updatedAt
- SupplierOrderLine
  - id
  - orderId
  - itemId/ingredientId (referência ao catálogo existente)
  - itemNameSnapshot
  - qty
  - unit
  - notes
  - position

Se já existir “PurchaseOrder/Order” no sistema, usar essa entidade e apenas adicionar campos/snapshots necessários para suportar o mailto + histórico.

NOVA ABA: UI/UX (MOBILE FIRST)
Criar rota/página “Encomendas” com 2 sub-vistas:
A) Lista/Histórico
- listar encomendas (data, fornecedor, nº itens, status)
- filtros básicos (fornecedor, datas, status) se for simples
- abrir detalhe

B) Nova Encomenda
- Step 1: selecionar fornecedor (dropdown/search)
- Step 2: adicionar linhas (mobile-friendly)
  - Em mobile: usar cards por linha em vez de tabela larga
  - Em desktop: pode usar tabela existente do sistema se for padrão
- Cada linha:
  - selecionar ingrediente/produto (search)
  - qty
  - unit (dropdown; default = unidade do item se existir)
  - notes (opcional)
- Botão “Gerar email” (ou gerar automaticamente)
- Preview do email (subject + body) editável (textarea)
- Botões finais:
  1) “Enviar pelo meu email” (abre mailto)
  2) “Copiar email” (fallback, copia subject+body para clipboard)

MAILTO (OBRIGATÓRIO E ROBUSTO)
Implementar utilitário `buildMailtoLink(to, subject, body)`:
- usar URLSearchParams e encoding correto
- quebras de linha com `\n`
- to = supplier.email
- validar supplier.email existe; se não existir bloquear envio com erro claro

IMPORTANTE: LIMITES DO MAILTO
- Alguns clientes limitam tamanho do URL. Implementar proteção:
  - se `mailtoLink.length` exceder um limite conservador (ex: 1800–2000 chars),
    então mostrar aviso e sugerir “Copiar email” (não abrir mailto automaticamente).
  - Mesmo assim permitir tentar abrir se o user insistir (opcional), mas default é o fallback.

GERAÇÃO DE EMAIL (pt-PT)
Template:
Assunto: “Encomenda – {NOME_RESTAURANTE} – {DATA}”
Corpo:
Olá {Fornecedor (opcional)},
Segue a nossa encomenda:
- Item — qty unit (notes)
Entrega pretendida: {data ou texto se existir}
Obrigado,
{Nome do restaurante}

O nome do restaurante deve vir do tenant/empresa já existente.

HISTÓRICO / TRACKING
Ao clicar “Enviar pelo meu email”:
1) Garantir que a encomenda está guardada com snapshot do subject/body (imutável)
2) Atualizar status para READY_TO_SEND
3) Abrir `window.location.href = mailtoLink`
4) Após click, atualizar status para OPENED_MAIL_CLIENT (best effort, não bloquear se falhar)
NOTA: não há “SENT” porque não há confirmação real.

PERMISSÕES
- Criar permissão/role compatível com o sistema (ex: “purchases:write” ou semelhante)
- Só users autorizados veem a aba e podem criar encomendas

ENTREGÁVEL
- Adicionar a nova aba ao menu
- Implementar páginas “Histórico” + “Nova encomenda” + “Detalhe”
- Implementar schema/migrations se necessário
- Implementar services/repositories seguindo padrões do projeto
- Garantir responsivo/mobile
- Garantir lint/build ok
- Adicionar notas rápidas no README (se for padrão) explicando que envio é via mailto e não há confirmação de envio

COMEÇA AGORA
1) Faz scan ao repo e lista os ficheiros onde vais mexer (nav/menu, routes, models, services, pages)
2) Reutiliza o máximo possível
3) Implementa incrementalmente: data model -> UI Nova Encomenda -> mailto -> histórico

    if (!wastingBatch) return;
    
    // Validate waste quantity
    const quantityValue = parseFloat(wasteQuantity);
    if (isNaN(quantityValue) || quantityValue <= 0) {
      toast.error(t("errorInvalidQuantity"));
      return;

    if (quantityValue > currentRemaining) {

1. **Redeploy no Vercel** a partir do commit mais recente:
   - Vercel → projeto → **Deployments**
   - No deployment mais recente (que deve ser o **b2971d0**), clica nos três pontos → **Redeploy**
   - Ou: **Deployments** → **Redeploy** no deployment que está em "main" / no topo da lista

   - `git commit --allow-empty -m "chore: trigger redeploy" && git push`

[Tool call] write
  file_path: c:\projects\validade-clean\lib\theme.ts
  contents: /**
 * Tamiini Brand Color Theme
 * Centralized color tokens for the entire application
 * 
 * Brand Guidelines:
 * - Primary: #aa3f2f
 * - Background/Cream: #f9e8d1
 * - Text/Dark: #572d1b
 * - Success Green: #375d33
 * - Soft Green: #e2e7bd
 * - Warning Yellow: #fde693
 * - Urgent Orange: #e9973e
 */

/**
 * Convert hex color to HSL format for CSS variables
 * Returns string in format "H S% L%" (without hsl() wrapper)
 */
function hexToHsl(hex: string): string {
  // Remove # if present
  hex = hex.replace('#', '');
  
  // Parse RGB
  const r = parseInt(hex.substring(0, 2), 16) / 255;
  const g = parseInt(hex.substring(2, 4), 16) / 255;
  const b = parseInt(hex.substring(4, 6), 16) / 255;

  const max = Math.max(r, g, b);
  const min = Math.min(r, g, b);
  let h = 0;
  let s = 0;
  const l = (max + min) / 2;

  if (max !== min) {
    const d = max - min;
    s = l > 0.5 ? d / (2 - max - min) : d / (max + min);
    
    switch (max) {
      case r:
        h = ((g - b) / d + (g < b ? 6 : 0)) / 6;
        break;
      case g:
        h = ((b - r) / d + 2) / 6;
        break;
      case b:
        h = ((r - g) / d + 4) / 6;
        break;
    }
  }

  h = Math.round(h * 360);
  s = Math.round(s * 100);
  const lPercent = Math.round(l * 100);

  return `${h} ${s}% ${lPercent}%`;
}

export const brandColors = {
  // Primary brand color
  primary: '#aa3f2f',
  // Background/Cream
  background: '#f9e8d1',
  // Text/Dark
  textDark: '#572d1b',
  // Success Green
  success: '#375d33',
  // Soft Green (for success backgrounds)
  successSoft: '#e2e7bd',
  // Warning Yellow
  warning: '#fde693',
  // Urgent Orange
  urgent: '#e9973e',
} as const;

/**
 * HSL values for CSS variables
 */
export const brandColorsHSL = {
  primary: hexToHsl(brandColors.primary),
  background: hexToHsl(brandColors.background),
  textDark: hexToHsl(brandColors.textDark),
  success: hexToHsl(brandColors.success),
  successSoft: hexToHsl(brandColors.successSoft),
  warning: hexToHsl(brandColors.warning),
  urgent: hexToHsl(brandColors.urgent),
} as const;

/**
 * Semantic color tokens for use in components
 */
export const theme = {
  brand: {
    primary: brandColors.primary,
    background: brandColors.background,
    textDark: brandColors.textDark,
  },
  status: {
    success: brandColors.success,
    successSoft: brandColors.successSoft,
    warning: brandColors.warning,
    urgent: brandColors.urgent,
    danger: brandColors.primary, // Use primary as danger/expired
  },
} as const;

## Resumo da atualização de cores

### Onde os tokens estão definidos

1. `lib/theme.ts` — Ficheiro centralizado com:
   - Cores da marca em hex (#aa3f2f, #f9e8d1, etc.)
   - Função `hexToHsl()` para conversão
   - Tokens semânticos exportados

2. `app/globals.css` — Variáveis CSS atualizadas:
   - `--primary`: #aa3f2f (Primary)
   - `--background`: #f9e8d1 (Background/Cream)
   - `--foreground`: #572d1b (Text/Dark)
   - `--destructive`: #aa3f2f (Danger/Expired usa primary)
   - `--urgent`, `--urgent-soft`: #e9973e (Urgent Orange)
   - `--warning`, `--warning-soft`: #fde693 (Warning Yellow)
   - `--success`, `--success-soft`: #375d33 / #e2e7bd (Success Green)

3. `tailwind.config.js` — Cores de status adicionadas:
   - `urgent`, `urgent-soft`
   - `warning`, `warning-soft`

B: 241/255 = 0.945

Max = 0.992 (R)
Min = 0.945 (B)
L = (0.992 + 0.945) / 2 = 0.9685 = 96.85%

Delta = 0.992 - 0.945 = 0.047
S = delta / (2 - max - min) = 0.047 / (2 - 0.992 - 0.945) = 0.047 / 0.063 = 0.746 = 74.6%

H: R é max
H = ((G - B) / delta) / 6 = ((0.969 - 0.945) / 0.047) / 6 = (0.024 / 0.047) / 6 = 0.5106 / 6 = 0.0851
H = 0.0851 * 360 = 30.64°

Então: 31 75% 97%
</think>
Atualizando os tokens e componentes. Adicionando a cor surface (#fdf7f1):

Para substituir o logo, podes enviar o SVG de uma destas formas:

**Opção 1:** Cola o código SVG diretamente aqui (entre tags `<svg>...</svg>`)

**Opção 2:** Coloca o ficheiro SVG na pasta `public/` e diz-me o nome do ficheiro (ex: `logo-novo.svg`)

**Opção 3:** Se já tens o SVG guardado, diz-me o caminho completo

Depois de receberes o SVG, faço:
1. Remover o SVG atual de `logo-svg.tsx`
2. Remover o texto "Tamiini" do cabeçalho (`showText={false}`)
3. Substituir pelo novo SVG

Envia o SVG quando estiveres pronto.

interface LogoSvgProps {
  className?: string;
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl";
}

/**
 * Inline SVG Logo Component - Optimized for FCP
 * Using inline SVG eliminates HTTP request and improves First Contentful Paint
 */
export function LogoSvg({ className, size = "md" }: LogoSvgProps) {
  const sizeClasses = {
    sm: "h-8 w-8",
    md: "h-12 w-12",
    lg: "h-16 w-16 md:h-20 md:w-20",
    xl: "h-24 w-24 md:h-32 md:w-32",
  };

  return (
    <svg 
      className={cn("flex-shrink-0", sizeClasses[size], className)}
      viewBox="0 0 518 518" 
      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      aria-label="Tamiini"
    >
  new_string: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

  className?: string;

 * Inline SVG Logo Component - Optimized for FCP

    lg: "h-12 w-auto md:h-16 md:w-auto",

[Tool call] write
  file_path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\logo-svg.tsx
  contents: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface LogoSvgProps {
  className?: string;
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl";
}

/**
 * Inline SVG Logo Component - Optimized for FCP
 * Using inline SVG eliminates HTTP request and improves First Contentful Paint
 */
export function LogoSvg({ className, size = "md" }: LogoSvgProps) {
  // Logo is horizontal (169.09 x 50.14), so we need to maintain aspect ratio
  const sizeClasses = {
    sm: "h-6 w-auto", // Maintain aspect ratio for horizontal logo
    md: "h-8 w-auto",
    lg: "h-12 w-auto md:h-16 md:w-auto",
    xl: "h-16 w-auto md:h-20 md:w-auto",
  };

  return (
    <svg 
      className={cn("flex-shrink-0", sizeClasses[size], className)}
      viewBox="0 0 169.09 50.14" 
      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      aria-label="Tamiini"
    >
      <defs>
        <style>{`.cls-1{fill:#aa3f2f;}.cls-2{fill:none;stroke:#aa3f2f;stroke-miterlimit:10;stroke-width:.75px;}`}</style>
      </defs>

#fdf7f1: R=253, G=247, B=241
R/255=0.992, G/255=0.969, B/255=0.945
Max=0.992, Min=0.945, L=(0.992+0.945)/2=0.9685
S: (0.992-0.945)/(1-|2*0.9685-1|) = 0.047/0.937 = 0.05 so S=5%
H: R is max, (G-B)/(Max-Min) = (0.969-0.945)/0.047 = 0.51, so H = 0.51*60 = 30.6° (roughly 31)

H = 30°, S ≈ 67%, L ≈ 97%  (some converters say 31 75% 97%)

[Tool call] Write
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\lib\tour-config.ts
  contents: /**
 * In-app guided tutorial (product tour) configuration.
 * Steps run only in the authenticated area.
 */

export const TOUR_STORAGE_KEY = "tamiini_tour_seen";

export interface TourStep {
  id: string;
  /** CSS selector for the element to highlight (e.g. [data-tour="dashboard"]) */
  target: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  /** Route to navigate to before showing this step (e.g. /definicoes). Done when entering step. */
  navigateTo?: string;
  /** For settings tabs: trigger click on this selector when entering step so tab is visible */
  triggerClick?: boolean;
}

export const TOUR_STEPS: TourStep[] = [
  {
    id: "dashboard",
    target: "[data-tour=\"dashboard\"]",
    title: "Visão Geral",
    description:
      "Aqui vê o estado do seu stock: expirado, urgente, atenção e OK. Use estes cartões para aceder rapidamente aos produtos em cada estado.",
  },
  {
    id: "nav-settings",
    target: "[data-tour=\"nav-settings\"]",
    title: "Definições",
    description:
      "Antes de usar o sistema, configure o seu restaurante nas Definições. Clique em Seguinte para ir até lá.",
    navigateTo: "/definicoes",
  },
  {
    id: "settings-general",
    target: "[data-tour=\"settings-general\"]",
    title: "Geral",
    description:
      "Indique o nome do restaurante. Assim identifica o estabelecimento em relatórios e nas encomendas.",
  },
  {
    id: "settings-email",
    target: "[data-tour=\"settings-email\"]",
    title: "Email para encomendas",
    description:
      "Ligue o email que usa para falar com fornecedores. As encomendas passam a ser enviadas a partir da aplicação e as respostas chegam ao seu correio.",
  },
  {
    id: "settings-categories",
    target: "[data-tour=\"settings-categories\"]",
    title: "Categorias",
    description:
      "Ajuste os dias de aviso por categoria (por exemplo, congelados vs frescos). Cada tipo de produto pode ter prazos diferentes.",
    triggerClick: true,
  },
  {
    id: "settings-locations",
    target: "[data-tour=\"settings-locations\"]",
    title: "Localizações",
    description:
      "Defina onde guarda os produtos: despensa, frigorífico, arca, etc. Assim organiza o stock por localização física.",
    triggerClick: true,
  },
];

- When step has navigateTo, on "Seguinte" we need to navigate and then advance. So the "next" action for step index 1 should: navigate to /definicoes, then after a short delay set stepIndex to 2. So the logic can be: when user clicks "Seguinte", if current step has navigateTo, call router.push(navigateTo) and then setTimeout(() => setStepIndex(i+1), 400). Otherwise just setStepIndex(i+1).
- When entering a step that has triggerClick, we click the target element (the tab trigger) so the tab content is visible.

  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\tour\tour-context.tsx
  contents: "use client";

import React, { createContext, useCallback, useContext, useMemo, useState } from "react";
import { useRouter } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TOUR_STEPS, TOUR_STORAGE_KEY } from "@/lib/tour-config";

interface TourContextValue {
  isOpen: boolean;
  stepIndex: number;
  steps: typeof TOUR_STEPS;
  openTour: () => void;
  closeTour: () => void;
  next: () => void;
  prev: () => void;
  skip: () => void;
  finish: () => void;
  runFirstTimeIfNeeded: () => void;
}

const TourContext = createContext<TourContextValue | null>(null);

export function useTour() {
  const ctx = useContext(TourContext);
  if (!ctx) return null;
  return ctx;
}

export function TourProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const router = useRouter();
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
  const [stepIndex, setStepIndex] = useState(0);

  const markSeen = useCallback(() => {
    if (typeof window !== "undefined") {
      try {
        window.localStorage.setItem(TOUR_STORAGE_KEY, "true");
      } catch (_) {}
    }
  }, []);

  const closeTour = useCallback(() => {
    setIsOpen(false);
  }, []);

  const openTour = useCallback(() => {
    setStepIndex(0);
    setIsOpen(true);
  }, []);

  const finish = useCallback(() => {
    markSeen();
    closeTour();
  }, [markSeen, closeTour]);

  const skip = useCallback(() => {
    markSeen();
    closeTour();
  }, [markSeen, closeTour]);

  const next = useCallback(() => {
    const step = TOUR_STEPS[stepIndex];
    const isLast = stepIndex >= TOUR_STEPS.length - 1;

    if (isLast) {
      finish();
      return;
    }

    if (step?.navigateTo) {
      router.push(step.navigateTo as any);
      setTimeout(() => setStepIndex((i) => i + 1), 350);
      return;
    }

    setStepIndex((i) => Math.min(i + 1, TOUR_STEPS.length - 1));
  }, [stepIndex, router, finish]);

  const prev = useCallback(() => {
    setStepIndex((i) => Math.max(0, i - 1));
  }, []);

  const runFirstTimeIfNeeded = useCallback(() => {
    if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
    try {
      const seen = window.localStorage.getItem(TOUR_STORAGE_KEY);
      if (seen === "true") return;
      setStepIndex(0);
      setIsOpen(true);
    } catch (_) {}
  }, []);

  const value = useMemo<TourContextValue>(
    () => ({
      isOpen,
      stepIndex,
      steps: TOUR_STEPS,
      openTour,
      closeTour,
      next,
      prev,
      skip,
      finish,
      runFirstTimeIfNeeded,
    }),
    [isOpen, stepIndex, openTour, closeTour, next, prev, skip, finish, runFirstTimeIfNeeded]
  );

  return <TourContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</TourContext.Provider>;
}

- Buttons: Anterior, Seguinte (or Concluir on last), Saltar

[Tool call] Write
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\tour\tour-overlay.tsx
  contents: "use client";

import React, { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
import { useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, X } from "lucide-react";

export function TourOverlay() {
  const tour = useTour();
  const [targetRect, setTargetRect] = useState<DOMRect | null>(null);
  const [tooltipPosition, setTooltipPosition] = useState<{ top: number; left: number } | null>(null);
  const tooltipRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  if (!tour || !tour.isOpen) return null;

  const { stepIndex, steps, next, prev, skip, finish } = tour;
  const step = steps[stepIndex];
  const isFirst = stepIndex === 0;
  const isLast = stepIndex >= steps.length - 1;

  // When step has triggerClick, click the target (e.g. tab trigger) so content is visible
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!step?.triggerClick || typeof document === "undefined") return;
    const el = document.querySelector(step.target);
    if (el && el instanceof HTMLElement) {
      el.click();
    }
  }, [step?.id, step?.triggerClick, step?.target]);

  // Measure target and position tooltip
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!step || typeof document === "undefined") return;

    const measure = () => {
      const el = document.querySelector(step.target);
      if (el && el instanceof HTMLElement) {
        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
        setTargetRect(rect);
        el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "center" });
        // Position tooltip below or above target, centered horizontally
        const gap = 12;
        const tooltipW = 320;
        const left = Math.max(16, Math.min(rect.left + rect.width / 2 - tooltipW / 2, window.innerWidth - tooltipW - 16));
        const top = rect.bottom + gap;
        if (top + 200 > window.innerHeight - 24) {
          setTooltipPosition({ left, top: rect.top - 220 });
        } else {
          setTooltipPosition({ left, top });
        }
      } else {
        setTargetRect(null);
        setTooltipPosition({
          left: Math.max(16, window.innerWidth / 2 - 160),
          top: Math.max(16, window.innerHeight / 2 - 120),
        });
      }
    };

    measure();
    const t = setTimeout(measure, 100);
    window.addEventListener("resize", measure);
    return () => {
      clearTimeout(t);
      window.removeEventListener("resize", measure);
    };
  }, [step?.target, stepIndex]);

  const handleNext = () => {
    if (isLast) finish();
    else next();
  };

  return (
    <div className="fixed inset-0 z-[100] pointer-events-none" aria-modal="true" role="dialog" aria-label="Tutorial">
      {/* Dimmed backdrop - allow clicks to pass through to "skip" behavior; we use a visible overlay that doesn't block */}
      <div
        className="absolute inset-0 bg-black/50 pointer-events-auto"
        onClick={skip}
        aria-hidden="true"
      />
      {/* Spotlight cutout around target */}
      {targetRect && (
        <div
          className="absolute pointer-events-none border-2 border-primary rounded-lg shadow-[0_0_0_9999px_rgba(0,0,0,0.5)]"
          style={{
            top: targetRect.top - 6,
            left: targetRect.left - 6,
            width: targetRect.width + 12,
            height: targetRect.height + 12,
          }}
        />
      )}
      {/* Tooltip - must be pointer-events-auto so buttons work */}
      {step && tooltipPosition && (
        <div
          ref={tooltipRef}
          className="absolute z-[101] w-[min(320px,calc(100vw-32px)) rounded-xl bg-card border border-border shadow-lg p-4 pointer-events-auto"
          style={{
            left: tooltipPosition.left,
            top: tooltipPosition.top,
          }}
        >
          <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-2 mb-2">
            <h3 className="text-base font-semibold text-foreground">{step.title}</h3>
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={skip}
              className="p-1 rounded-md text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:bg-muted"
              aria-label="Fechar"
            >
              <X className="h-4 w-4" />
            </button>
          </div>
          <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-4">{step.description}</p>
          <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2">
            <div className="flex gap-2">
              <Button type="button" variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={prev} disabled={isFirst}>
                <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
                Anterior
              </Button>
              <Button type="button" variant="default" size="sm" onClick={handleNext}>
                {isLast ? "Concluir" : "Seguinte"}
                {!isLast && <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />}
              </Button>
            </div>
            <Button type="button" variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={skip} className="text-muted-foreground">
              Saltar
            </Button>
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

assistant:
<think>
The spotlight uses a box-shadow trick: box-shadow: 0 0 0 9999px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) on the highlighted element creates a dark overlay with a "hole". But I'm drawing a div that's the same size as the target - the "hole" would need to be the inverse. So the overlay is the full-screen dim, and I need a "hole" where the target is. The common approach is: full screen overlay with background, then a div that's the same size/position as the target with a huge box-shadow that extends in all directions - but that would create a dark rectangle, not a hole. The hole effect is done by having the overlay as the dark layer and then a div positioned like the target with a transparent background and box-shadow: 0 0 0 9999px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) - no, that would add more dark. The correct trick: overlay is semi-transparent black. Then we have a "spotlight" div that is positioned exactly over the target, with the same size, and we use box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 9999px ... no. The standard approach is: one full-screen div with background rgba(0,0,0,0.5). Then a second div that's the "cutout" - same position/size as target - with background transparent and box-shadow: 0 0 0 9999px rgba(0,0,0,0.5). That way the small rectangle is "transparent" and the rest of the screen is dark. So the "cutout" div is the target size, has no background, and has a huge box-shadow that fills the screen - so everywhere except the div itself is covered. So we need the overlay to be the cutout div: a div the size of the target, with box-shadow: 0 0 0 9999px rgba(0,0,0,0.5). That creates a dark ring with a "hole" in the middle (the div itself). Let me fix the overlay.
</think>
Corrigindo o spotlight: usar um único div do tamanho do alvo com box-shadow para criar o “buraco”.

  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\authenticated-tour.tsx
  contents: "use client";

import React, { useEffect } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";

const PUBLIC_ROUTES = ["/", "/acesso", "/onboarding"];

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const isAuthenticated = !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname);

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

---

## Tutorial guiado (product tour) – implementado

### 1. **Só na área autenticada**
- O botão **?** e o tutorial **só aparecem** quando o utilizador não está em `/`, `/acesso` ou `/onboarding`.
- Em ecrã de PIN/login não há botão nem tour.

### 2. **Botão de ajuda “?”**
- **Componente:** `components/tour/help-button.tsx`
- Posição: fixo no canto inferior direito (no mobile ligeiramente à esquerda para não tapar o FAB).
- Cor: `#aa3f2f`.
- Tooltip/título: "Ajuda".
- Ao clicar, abre o tutorial do início.

### 3. **Tutorial (overlay)**
- **Componentes:** `components/tour/tour-context.tsx`, `components/tour/tour-overlay.tsx`
- Fundo escurecido; destaque (spotlight) no elemento alvo; tooltip com título e descrição.
- Botões: **Anterior**, **Seguinte** / **Concluir**, **Saltar**.
- Se o alvo não existir (ex.: passo “Dashboard” em Definições), o tooltip aparece ao centro e o fluxo continua.

### 4. **Primeira vez**
- **Chave em localStorage:** `tamiini_tour_seen`.
- Na **primeira entrada** na área autenticada (após PIN com sucesso), o tutorial começa automaticamente uma vez (com ~600 ms de atraso).
- Depois de **Concluir** ou **Saltar**, grava-se `tamiini_tour_seen` e o tour deixa de abrir sozinho; só volta a abrir ao clicar em “?”.

### 5. **Fluxo (6 passos)**
- **1. Visão Geral** – Destaca o conteúdo do dashboard; explica expirado / urgente / atenção / OK.
- **2. Definições** – Destaca o link “Definições” na nav; ao clicar “Seguinte” navega para `/definicoes`.
- **3. Geral** – Destaca o separador Geral; explica o nome do restaurante.
- **4. Email para encomendas** – Destaca o bloco de email; explica a ligação do email para encomendas.
- **5. Categorias** – Abre o separador Categorias e destaca-o; explica os dias de aviso por categoria.
- **6. Localizações** – Abre o separador Localizações e destaca-o; explica despensa, frigorífico, etc.

### 6. **Ficheiros criados/alterados**
- **Novos:**  
  `lib/tour-config.ts`, `components/tour/tour-context.tsx`, `components/tour/tour-overlay.tsx`, `components/tour/help-button.tsx`, `components/authenticated-tour.tsx`
- **Alterados:**  
  `app/[locale]/layout.tsx` (wrap com `AuthenticatedTour`),  
  `components/dashboard-content.tsx` (`data-tour="dashboard"`),  
  `components/main-nav.tsx` (`data-tour="nav-settings"` nos links Definições),  
  `components/settings-content.tsx` (`data-tour` nos separadores Geral/Categorias/Localizações e no Card de email).

Textos em PT-PT, tom de onboarding moderno, sem alterar lógica de negócio, layout, rotas ou fluxos principais.

assistant:
<think>
ScrollArea is only used/defined in scroll-area.tsx - no other file imports it. So we can replace the implementation with a simple native scroll div that keeps the same component names and similar props. That way if something does import ScrollArea later, it still works.

I'll rewrite the component to use a simple div with overflow-auto (and optional ScrollBar as a visual bar). The Radix Root accepts className and children. So:

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

function ScrollArea({
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="scroll-area"
      className={cn("relative overflow-auto", className)}
      {...props}
    >
      {children}
    </div>
  )
}

function ScrollBar({
  className,
  orientation = "vertical",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { orientation?: "vertical" | "horizontal" }) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="scroll-area-scrollbar"
      className={cn(
        "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
        orientation === "vertical" && "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
        orientation === "horizontal" && "h-2.5 w-full border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
        className
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar }
```

But wait - the original ScrollArea has a Viewport and ScrollBar inside. So the API is <ScrollArea><content></ScrollArea>. The ScrollBar was inside and automatically shown. So consumers might use <ScrollArea className="...">...</ScrollArea> and expect scrolling. So a simple div with overflow-auto is enough. The ScrollBar component might be used somewhere - the grep only found it in the same file (used inside ScrollArea). So I'll provide a minimal ScrollBar that does nothing visible (or a thin track) so the API is preserved. Let me keep ScrollBar as an optional decorative element - in the simple implementation we can just not render it or render a placeholder div for compatibility. Actually the original always renders <ScrollBar /> inside ScrollArea. So I'll do:

ScrollArea = div with overflow-auto, and optionally render a scrollbar (we can use CSS overflow: overlay or just overflow-auto and the browser shows scrollbar). So the simplest is just a div with overflow-auto. ScrollBar can be a no-op or a thin div for API compatibility.
</think>
Reescrevendo o `ScrollArea` para não depender do Radix, mantendo a mesma API.

[Tool call] Write
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\ui\scroll-area.tsx
  contents: "use client";

import * as React from "react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface ScrollAreaProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  children?: React.ReactNode;
}

function ScrollArea({ className, children, ...props }: ScrollAreaProps) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="scroll-area"
      className={cn("relative overflow-auto rounded-[inherit]", className)}
      {...props}
    >
      {children}
    </div>
  );
}

interface ScrollBarProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  orientation?: "vertical" | "horizontal";
}

function ScrollBar({
  className,
  orientation = "vertical",
  ...props
}: ScrollBarProps) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="scroll-area-scrollbar"
      className={cn(
        "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
        orientation === "vertical" &&
          "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
        orientation === "horizontal" &&
          "h-2.5 w-full flex-col border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
        className
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar };

import React, { useEffect } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";

const PUBLIC_ROUTES = ["/", "/acesso", "/onboarding"];

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const isAuthenticated = !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname);

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}
  new_string: "use client";

import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";

const PUBLIC_ROUTES = ["/", "/acesso", "/onboarding"];

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const pathname = usePathname();

  useEffect(() => {
    setMounted(true);
  }, []);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  const isAuthenticated = !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname ?? "");

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

  </TourProvider>
</TourErrorBoundary>

    {children}  // always show
    <TourErrorBoundary fallback={null}>
      <TourProvider>
        <TourAutoRun />
        {null}
        <TourOverlay />
        <TourHelpButton />
      </TourProvider>

Criando um error boundary para o tour e envolvendo-o para que a app não caia.

[Tool call] Write
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\components\tour\tour-error-boundary.tsx
  contents: "use client";

import React from "react";

interface Props {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  fallback: React.ReactNode;
}

interface State {
  hasError: boolean;
}

/**
 * Catches errors in the tour UI so the rest of the app keeps working.
 */
export class TourErrorBoundary extends React.Component<Props, State> {
  constructor(props: Props) {
    super(props);
    this.state = { hasError: false };
  }

  static getDerivedStateFromError(): State {
    return { hasError: true };
  }

  componentDidCatch(error: Error, info: React.ErrorInfo) {
    console.error("[Tour]", error, info.componentStack);
  }

  render() {
    if (this.state.hasError) {
      return this.props.fallback;
    }
    return this.props.children;
  }
}

import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const pathname = usePathname();

  useEffect(() => {
    setMounted(true);
  }, []);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  const isAuthenticated = !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname ?? "");

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;

  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />

  new_string: "use client";

import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";
import { TourErrorBoundary } from "@/components/tour/tour-error-boundary";

const PUBLIC_ROUTES = ["/", "/acesso", "/onboarding"];

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

function TourShell({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const [pathname, setPathname] = useState<string | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    setMounted(true);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!mounted) return;
    try {
      const { usePathname: getPathname } = require("@/i18n/navigation");
      const current = getPathname();
      setPathname(typeof current === "string" ? current : null);
    } catch {
      setPathname(null);
    }
  }, [mounted]);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  const isAuthenticated =
    pathname !== null && !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname);

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourErrorBoundary fallback={null}>
      <TourShell>{children}</TourShell>
    </TourErrorBoundary>
  );
}

import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
import { TourProvider, useTour } from "@/components/tour/tour-context";
import { TourOverlay } from "@/components/tour/tour-overlay";
import { TourHelpButton } from "@/components/tour/help-button";
import { TourErrorBoundary } from "@/components/tour/tour-error-boundary";

const PUBLIC_ROUTES = ["/", "/acesso", "/onboarding"];

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

function TourShell({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <TourAutoRun />
      {children}
      <TourOverlay />
      <TourHelpButton />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const [pathname, setPathname] = useState<string | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    setMounted(true);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!mounted) return;
    try {
      const { usePathname: getPathname } = require("@/i18n/navigation");
      const current = getPathname();
      setPathname(typeof current === "string" ? current : null);
    } catch {
      setPathname(null);
    }
  }, [mounted]);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  const isAuthenticated =
    pathname !== null && !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname);

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourErrorBoundary fallback={null}>
      <TourShell>{children}</TourShell>
    </TourErrorBoundary>
  );
}

function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;

    setMounted(true);

assistant:
<think>
Error boundaries don't catch errors in the same component that renders them - they catch errors in the tree below. So when TourShell throws (or any child), TourErrorBoundary will catch it and render fallback={null}. But then we're not rendering {children} anymore - we're rendering <TourErrorBoundary><TourShell>{children}</TourShell></TourErrorBoundary>. So TourShell contains {children}. So when we catch, we render null and we lose the children! I need to fix this: when the boundary catches, we must still show children. So the structure should be:

return (
  <>
    {children}
    <TourErrorBoundary fallback={null}>
      <TourProvider>
        <TourAutoRun />
        <TourOverlay />
        <TourHelpButton />
      </TourProvider>
    </TourErrorBoundary>
  </>
);

So we always render children. The tour UI (provider + overlay + button) is in the boundary. If it throws, we render null for that part, but children are already there above. Perfect.

So we render the full dimmed overlay (the else branch) but no tooltip. So the user would see a dimmed screen with no tooltip - and the measure() would run and set tooltipPosition. So after a re-render we'd have the tooltip. Unless... the measure() or the useEffect throws. Let me check - we have if (!step) return null. steps[stepIndex] when stepIndex is 0 is steps[0] which exists. So step is defined. We then have two useEffects. During initial render we don't run those yet. So we return the JSX. The JSX uses targetRect (null), tooltipPosition (null), step (defined). So we render the backdrop (the else branch - full dim) and we don't render the tooltip (step && tooltipPosition is false). So we get a dimmed overlay with nothing in the center. So the error boundary is now in "hasError: true" state. So when the user clicks "?", openTour() sets isOpen to true, but the TourOverlay is inside the boundary which is in error state - so it renders fallback=null and never re-mounts the children. So the boundary doesn't re-render the children when state changes; once it's in error state it stays there until the boundary itself unmounts. So the overlay never shows!

That's the bug. Once the error boundary catches an error, it stays in hasError state and always renders fallback. So we need to either:
1. Reset the error boundary when the user clicks "?" (we'd need a key that changes when openTour is called, so the boundary remounts and tries again)

So we could have a wrapper component that reads tour.isOpen and passes it as key to the boundary. So when isOpen toggles, the boundary remounts and gets a fresh chance. Let me do that.

Actually simpler: use a key on the error boundary that's the stepIndex or isOpen. When user clicks "?", isOpen becomes true. If we have a component that wraps the boundary and passes key={tour.isOpen}, when isOpen changes the boundary remounts. So the children of the boundary (TourAutoRun, TourOverlay) remount. So we get a fresh try. Let me implement that - we need a component that uses useTour() and renders <TourErrorBoundary key={tour.isOpen ? export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {

  new_string: function TourAutoRun() {
  const tour = useTour();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!tour) return;
    const t = setTimeout(() => tour.runFirstTimeIfNeeded(), 600);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [tour]);
  return null;
}

/** Remonta o boundary quando o tour abre, para o overlay ter nova hipótese de renderizar */
function TourOverlayWithBoundary() {
  const tour = useTour();
  const key = tour?.isOpen ? "open" : "closed";
  return (
    <TourErrorBoundary key={key} fallback={null}>
      <TourAutoRun />
      <TourOverlay />
    </TourErrorBoundary>
  );
}

export function AuthenticatedTour({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
  const pathname = usePathname();

  useEffect(() => {
    setMounted(true);
  }, []);

  if (!mounted) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  const isAuthenticated = !PUBLIC_ROUTES.includes(pathname ?? "");

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <TourProvider>
      {children}
      <TourHelpButton />
      <TourOverlayWithBoundary />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

  old_string:   // Ordenar produtos do Stock Geral alfabeticamente - só quando generalStock mudar
  // Otimizado para performance: cache de nomes ordenados
  const generalStockProductNames = useMemo(() => {
    if (activeTab !== "general") return [];
    if (!generalStockComputed || Object.keys(generalStockComputed).length === 0) return [];
    try {
      // Criar array de nomes com displayName para ordenação mais eficiente
      const namesWithDisplay = Object.entries(generalStockComputed).map(([key, product]) => ({
        key,
        displayName: product.displayName || "",
      }));
      
      // Ordenar apenas uma vez
      namesWithDisplay.sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));
      
      // Retornar apenas as keys ordenadas
      return namesWithDisplay.map(item => item.key);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Error sorting general stock products:", error);
      return [];
    }
  }, [generalStockComputed, activeTab]);
  new_string:   // Ordenar produtos do Stock Geral alfabeticamente e filtrar por pesquisa
  const generalStockProductNames = useMemo(() => {
    if (activeTab !== "general") return [];
    if (!generalStockComputed || Object.keys(generalStockComputed).length === 0) return [];
    try {
      const namesWithDisplay = Object.entries(generalStockComputed).map(([key, product]) => ({
        key,
        displayName: product.displayName || "",
      }));

      namesWithDisplay.sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));

      let keys = namesWithDisplay.map((item) => item.key);

      if (searchQuery.trim()) {
        const query = searchQuery.toLowerCase();
        keys = namesWithDisplay
          .filter((item) => item.displayName.toLowerCase().includes(query))
          .map((item) => item.key);
      }

      return keys;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Error sorting general stock products:", error);
      return [];
    }
  }, [generalStockComputed, activeTab, searchQuery]);

/**
 * Shared search: returns true if product/display name matches the search query (case-insensitive).
 * Used by both batch-list and aggregated (general) stock views for consistent behaviour.
 */
export function productNameMatchesSearch(
  name: string | null | undefined,
  searchQuery: string
): boolean {
  if (!searchQuery.trim()) return true;
  if (!name) return false;
  const query = searchQuery.trim().toLowerCase();
  return name.toLowerCase().includes(query);
}

 * Agrupa batches por categoria.

[Tool call] StrReplace
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\app\actions.ts
  old_string: export async function deleteCategoryById(formData: FormData) {
  "use server";
  const categoryId = formData.get("categoryId")?.toString();
  if (!categoryId) {
    return {
      success: false,
      error: "Category ID required",
    };
  }
  return deleteCategory(categoryId);
}
  new_string: export async function updateCategoryName(categoryId: string, newName: string) {
  try {
    const tenantId = await getRestaurantIdFromSession();
    if (!tenantId) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Não autenticado. Por favor, faça login novamente.",
      };
    }

    const restaurant = await getRestaurantByTenantId(tenantId);
    const name = newName.trim();

    if (!name) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Por favor, forneça um nome para a categoria.",
      };
    }

    const category = await db.category.findFirst({
      where: { id: categoryId, restaurantId: restaurant.id },
    });

    if (!category) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Categoria não encontrada.",
      };
    }

    if (category.name === name) {
      revalidatePath("/definicoes", "page");
      revalidatePath("/settings", "page");
      return { success: true, message: "Nome mantido." };
    }

    const existingWithName = await db.category.findFirst({
      where: {
        restaurantId: restaurant.id,
        name,
        id: { not: categoryId },
      },
    });

    if (existingWithName) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: `Já existe uma categoria com o nome "${name}".`,
      };
    }

    await db.category.update({
      where: { id: categoryId, restaurantId: restaurant.id },
      data: { name },
    });

    revalidatePath("/definicoes", "page");
    revalidatePath("/settings", "page");
    revalidatePath("/stock", "page");

    return {
      success: true,
      message: "Nome da categoria atualizado.",
    };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error updating category name:", error);
    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Erro desconhecido ao renomear categoria.";
    return {
      success: false,
      error: errorMessage,
    };
  }
}

export async function deleteCategoryById(formData: FormData) {
  "use server";
  const categoryId = formData.get("categoryId")?.toString();
  if (!categoryId) {
    return {
      success: false,
      error: "Category ID required",
    };
  }
  return deleteCategory(categoryId);
}

    if (!editingCategoryId || !editingCategoryName.trim()) return;
    startTransition(async () => {
      try {
        const result = await updateCategoryName(editingCategoryId, editingCategoryName.trim());

Implementing normalizeForSearch:
- Accents: use string.normalize("NFD") then replace(/\p{Mark}/gu, "") or replace(/[\u0300-\u036f]/g, "").
- Lowercase: toLowerCase()
- Punctuation/special: replace with space - e.g. replace(/[\s\p{P}\p{S}]/gu, " ") or similar. Actually \p{P} is punctuation, \p{S} is symbols. Then collapse spaces: replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim().
- So order: trim, normalize NFD, remove combining marks, toLowerCase, replace punctuation/symbols with space, collapse spaces, trim again.

assistant:
<think>
I'll create lib/search-utils.ts with normalizeForSearch and optionally a textMatchesSearch. Then update stock-utils to use it, stock-view.tsx, new-supplier-order-form.tsx, and actions.ts.

normalizeForSearch(str: string): string
1. if !str return ""
2. str = str.trim()
3. str = str.normalize("NFD") 
4. str = str.replace(/\p{M}/gu, "")  // remove combining marks (accents) - \p{M} is "Mark"
5. str = str.toLowerCase()
6. str = str.replace(/[\p{P}\p{S}\p{Z}\s]+/gu, " ")  // punctuation, symbols, spaces -> single space. Actually \p{Z} is separator. Let me use [\p{P}\p{S}] and also \s for any whitespace -> " "
7. str = str.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim()
8. return str

Actually for "repeated or extra spaces" we collapse to one. For "punctuation / special characters" - do we remove them or replace with space? Replacing with space is safer so "açúcar" and "acucar" both become "acucar" (no space), and "foo-bar" could match "foo bar". I'll remove punctuation/special entirely so we don't introduce extra spaces that could break matching - actually the user said "insensitive to ... punctuation / special characters" so they shouldn't affect the match. So "açúcar" normalized = "acucar", "a-c-u-c-a-r" normalized could be "acucar" if we remove punctuation. So: replace punctuation/symbols with nothing (remove) or with space. If we replace with space then "açúcar" -> "acucar", "açúcar (branco)" -> "acucar branco". So when searching "acucar" we need "acucar branco".includes("acucar") -> true. If we remove punctuation: "açúcar (branco)" -> "acucar branco". Good. So: replace [\p{P}\p{S}] with " ", then collapse spaces. So step 6: str = str.replace(/[\p{P}\p{S}]/gu, " "); step 7: str = str.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
</think>

[Tool call] Write
  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\lib\search-utils.ts
  contents: /**
 * Normalizes a string for search comparison.
 * Makes matching insensitive to:
 * - accents / diacritics (e.g. "camarão" <-> "camarao")
 * - uppercase / lowercase
 * - punctuation and special characters (treated as ignorable / space)
 * - repeated or extra spaces (collapsed to single space, trimmed)
 *
 * Use this for BOTH the search input and the values being searched
 * so that "camarao" matches "camarão", "água" matches "agua", etc.
 */
export function normalizeForSearch(str: string | null | undefined): string {
  if (str == null || typeof str !== "string") return "";
  let s = str.trim();
  if (!s) return "";
  // Decompose accents (é -> e + combining acute) then remove combining marks
  s = s.normalize("NFD").replace(/\p{M}/gu, "");
  s = s.toLowerCase();
  // Replace punctuation and symbols with space, then collapse spaces
  s = s.replace(/[\p{P}\p{S}]/gu, " ").replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
  return s;
}

/**
 * Returns true if the given text matches the search query when both
 * are normalized (accent-, case-, punctuation-, and space-insensitive).
 * Empty query matches any text; null/undefined text never matches.
 */
export function textMatchesSearch(
  text: string | null | undefined,
  searchQuery: string
): boolean {
  const q = normalizeForSearch(searchQuery);
  if (!q) return true;
  const normalizedText = normalizeForSearch(text);
  if (!normalizedText) return false;
  return normalizedText.includes(q);
}

 * Shared search: returns true if product/display name matches the search query (case-insensitive).
 * Used by both batch-list and aggregated (general) stock views for consistent behaviour.
 */
export function productNameMatchesSearch(
  name: string | null | undefined,
  searchQuery: string
): boolean {
  if (!searchQuery.trim()) return true;
  if (!name) return false;
  const query = searchQuery.trim().toLowerCase();
  return name.toLowerCase().includes(query);
}
  new_string: import { textMatchesSearch } from "@/lib/search-utils";

/**
 * Shared search: returns true if product/display name matches the search query.
 * Matching is accent-, case-, punctuation-, and space-insensitive (see normalizeForSearch).
 * Used by both batch-list and aggregated (general) stock views for consistent behaviour.
 */
export function productNameMatchesSearch(
  name: string | null | undefined,
  searchQuery: string
): boolean {
  return textMatchesSearch(name, searchQuery);
}

  path: c:\projects\validade-clean\app\actions.ts
  old_string: export async function searchProductTemplatesByName(query: string) {
  const tenantId = await getRestaurantIdFromSession();
  if (!tenantId) return { success: false, error: "Unauthorized", data: [] as never[] };

  try {
    const normalizedQuery = String(query ?? "").trim();
    if (!normalizedQuery || normalizedQuery.length < 2) return { success: true, data: [] as never[] };

    // 1) Per-restaurant templates matching the query
    const templates = await db.productTemplate.findMany({
      where: {
        restaurantId: tenantId,
        name: { contains: normalizedQuery, mode: "insensitive" },
      },
      select: { name: true, unit: true, categoryId: true, locationId: true, barcode: true },
      take: 5,
      orderBy: { name: "asc" },
    });

    // 2) Global catalog fallback for the remaining slots
    const templateNames = new Set(templates.map((t) => t.name.toLowerCase()));
    const globalRaw = await db.globalProduct.findMany({
      where: { name: { contains: normalizedQuery, mode: "insensitive" } },
      select: { name: true, barcode: true },
      take: 10,
      orderBy: { name: "asc" },
    });

    const globalFiltered = globalRaw
      .filter((g) => !templateNames.has(g.name.toLowerCase()))
      .slice(0, Math.max(0, 5 - templates.length));

    // 3) For each global result, try to enrich with per-restaurant template data by name
    const enriched = await Promise.all(
      globalFiltered.map(async (g) => {
        const tpl = await db.productTemplate.findFirst({
          where: {
            restaurantId: tenantId,
            name: { equals: g.name, mode: "insensitive" },
          },
          select: { unit: true, categoryId: true, locationId: true },
        });
        return {
          name: g.name,
          barcode: g.barcode,
          unit: tpl?.unit ?? null,
          categoryId: tpl?.categoryId ?? null,
          locationId: tpl?.locationId ?? null,
          source: "global" as const,
        };
      })
    );

    return {
      success: true,
      data: [
        ...templates.map((t) => ({ ...t, source: "restaurant" as const })),
        ...enriched,
      ],
    };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error searching product templates by name:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "Error searching products", data: [] as never[] };
  }
}
  new_string: export async function searchProductTemplatesByName(query: string) {
  const tenantId = await getRestaurantIdFromSession();
  if (!tenantId) return { success: false, error: "Unauthorized", data: [] as never[] };

  try {
    const rawQuery = String(query ?? "").trim();
    if (!rawQuery || rawQuery.length < 2) return { success: true, data: [] as never[] };

    // 1) Per-restaurant templates: fetch more then filter with accent-/case-insensitive match
    const allTemplates = await db.productTemplate.findMany({
      where: { restaurantId: tenantId },
      select: { name: true, unit: true, categoryId: true, locationId: true, barcode: true },
      take: 200,
      orderBy: { name: "asc" },
    });
    const templates = allTemplates
      .filter((t) => textMatchesSearch(t.name, rawQuery))
      .slice(0, 5);

    // 2) Global catalog: fetch more then filter with same normalization
    const templateNames = new Set(templates.map((t) => t.name.toLowerCase()));
    const globalRaw = await db.globalProduct.findMany({
      select: { name: true, barcode: true },
      take: 100,
      orderBy: { name: "asc" },
    });
    const globalFiltered = globalRaw
      .filter((g) => textMatchesSearch(g.name, rawQuery) && !templateNames.has(g.name.toLowerCase()))
      .slice(0, Math.max(0, 5 - templates.length));

    // 3) Enrich global results with per-restaurant template data by name
    const enriched = await Promise.all(
      globalFiltered.map(async (g) => {
        const tpl = await db.productTemplate.findFirst({
          where: {
            restaurantId: tenantId,
            name: { equals: g.name, mode: "insensitive" },
          },
          select: { unit: true, categoryId: true, locationId: true },
        });
        return {
          name: g.name,
          barcode: g.barcode,
          unit: tpl?.unit ?? null,
          categoryId: tpl?.categoryId ?? null,
          locationId: tpl?.locationId ?? null,
          source: "global" as const,
        };
      })
    );

    return {
      success: true,
      data: [
        ...templates.map((t) => ({ ...t, source: "restaurant" as const })),
        ...enriched,
      ],
    };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error searching product templates by name:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "Error searching products", data: [] as never[] };
  }
}

All good. Let me provide a short summary to the user.

Summary of changes:

**1. Shared search utility – `lib/search-utils.ts`**
- **`normalizeForSearch(str)`** – One place for search normalization:
  - **Accents:** NFD + remove combining marks (e.g. `camarão` → `camarao`, `açúcar` → `acucar`).
  - **Case:** lowercased.
  - **Punctuation/symbols:** replaced with space.
  - **Spaces:** collapse runs to one and trim.
- **`textMatchesSearch(text, searchQuery)`** – Normalizes both sides and returns `normalizedText.includes(normalizedQuery)`; empty query matches any text.

**2. Stock search (all UIs)**
- **`lib/stock-utils.ts`** – `productNameMatchesSearch` now uses `textMatchesSearch` (no more only `.toLowerCase()`), so Stock list and Stock Geral both use the same accent/case/punctuation/space rules.
- **`components/stock-view.tsx`** – Replaced inline `toLowerCase().includes(query)` with `productNameMatchesSearch(batch.name, searchQuery)`.
- **`components/stock-view-simple.tsx`** – Already used `productNameMatchesSearch`; no code change, behaviour comes from the updated `productNameMatchesSearch`.

**3. Supplier order product picker**
- **`components/new-supplier-order-form.tsx`** – Product filter and exact match now use the shared logic:
  - Filter: `productNameMatchesSearch(p.name, inputValue) || productNameMatchesSearch(p.category?.name, inputValue)`.
  - Exact match: `normalizeForSearch(p.name) === normalizeForSearch(inputValue.trim())`.

**4. Server-side product search**
- **`app/actions.ts` – `searchProductTemplatesByName`** – No longer relies on Prisma `contains` (which is not accent-insensitive). It:
  - Loads more restaurant templates and global products.
  - Filters in JS with `textMatchesSearch(name, rawQuery)`.
  - Returns the same shape and limit (5 restaurant + up to 5 global).

So:
- **"camarao"** matches **"camarão"** (and the other way around).
- **"agua"** matches **"água"**, **"acucar"** matches **"açúcar"**.
- Matching is the same in Stock (both views), supplier order product picker, and the product name search used by the new-entry form. No UI or storage changes, only the search comparison logic, centralized in `lib/search-utils.ts`.

assistant:
<think>
Now I have a clear picture. The user can change the logo in two ways:

1. **Ficheiro de imagem (mais simples):** Substituir o ficheiro **`public/logo.svg`**. Este é usado quando o componente `Logo` é renderizado com `inline={false}` (ou seja, como `<img src="/logo.svg">`). O layout também faz preload de `/logo.svg`.

2. **Componente SVG inline:** O logo que aparece por defeito na app é o **`components/logo-svg.tsx`** — um SVG desenhado em código (marca "Tamiini" com viewBox 169.09 x 50.14). Quando `inline={true}` (que é o default), o `Logo` usa `<LogoSvg />` e não o ficheiro.

Olhando para o main-nav e outras páginas, usam `<Logo size="sm" showText={false} />` sem passar `inline`, então usam o default `inline=true`, ou seja **LogoSvg** (o SVG inline). Por isso na prática o que está a aparecer é o componente **logo-svg.tsx**.

Para mudar o logo, o utilizador pode:
- **Opção A:** Substituir **`public/logo.svg`** por um novo ficheiro (mesmo nome). Isto só afeta sítios que usem `inline={false}`. Como o default é `inline={true}`, a maioria dos usos mostra o LogoSvg. Então para mudar o que realmente aparece na nav e nas páginas, precisa de...
- **Opção B:** Alterar **`components/logo-svg.tsx`** para desenhar o novo logo, OU fazer o componente usar uma imagem em vez do SVG inline.
- **Opção C (mais simples para o utilizador):** Fazer o Logo usar sempre a imagem de `public/logo.svg` (mudar default para inline=false ou alterar o componente para sempre usar img quando há ficheiro). Assim o utilizador só substitui `public/logo.svg`.

A forma mais fácil para "mudar o logo" é dizer ao utilizador:
1. Onde está o logo atualmente: usado em `main-nav`, página inicial, choose-language; vem do componente `Logo` que por defeito usa o SVG inline em `logo-svg.tsx`.
2. Para trocar por uma imagem sua: colocar o ficheiro (ex: logo.svg ou logo.png) em **`public/`** e depois podemos alterar o componente Logo para usar essa imagem por defeito. Ou simplesmente substituir o conteúdo de `public/logo.svg` pelo novo logo.
3. Se quiser manter o sistema atual: editar o SVG em `components/logo-svg.tsx` (ou substituir o componente por uma img que aponte para /logo.svg).

Na verdade, no código atual, quando `inline=true` (default), usa LogoSvg. Quando inline=false, usa img src="/logo.svg". Então para o utilizador mudar o logo da forma mais fácil:
- **Substituir o ficheiro `public/logo.svg`** pelo novo logo (em SVG). Mas isso só afetaria usos com inline=false. Nenhum uso atual passa inline=false!
- Então atualmente TODOS os usos mostram o LogoSvg (inline). Para o novo logo em ficheiro funcionar, temos duas opções: (1) mudar o default de inline para false no Logo, assim passa a usar /logo.svg em todo o lado; (2) ou alterar logo-svg.tsx para carregar e exibir /logo.svg.

A resposta mais direta ao utilizador: "Onde ponho o logo?" — Podes colocar o teu logo em **`public/logo.svg`** (substitui o ficheiro que está lá). Depois é preciso garantir que a app usa esse ficheiro: atualmente a navegação e as páginas usam um SVG desenhado em código em `components/logo-svg.tsx`. Para passares a usar o teu ficheiro em todo o lado, ou mudamos o componente para usar a imagem por defeito, ou editas `logo-svg.tsx` para referenciar /logo.svg. ---

**Onde pôr o ficheiro do novo logo**

- Coloca o teu ficheiro em: **`public/logo.svg`**  
  (ou, por exemplo, `public/logo.png` se for PNG; o nome pode ser outro se alterarmos o código.)

Substitui o `logo.svg` que lá está pelo teu, mantendo o nome **`logo.svg`**, para ser mais simples.

---

**Como é que o logo é usado agora**

- O componente **`components/logo.tsx`** é o que mostra o logo na app (nav, página inicial, etc.).
- Por defeito ele usa o **SVG em código** em **`components/logo-svg.tsx`** (logo “Tamiini”), não o ficheiro da pasta `public`.
- O ficheiro **`public/logo.svg`** só é usado se o `Logo` for chamado com `inline={false}` (o que atualmente não acontece em lado nenhum).

Por isso: para **mudar o logo** sem mexer no SVG em código, o mais simples é:

1. **Pôr o teu logo em** `public/logo.svg` (substituir o ficheiro atual).
2. **Fazer a app usar sempre esse ficheiro** – por exemplo, alterar o `Logo` para usar por defeito a imagem `/logo.svg` em vez do SVG inline.

import { LogoSvg } from "./logo-svg";

interface LogoProps {
  className?: string;
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl";
  showText?: boolean;
  inline?: boolean; // Use inline SVG for better FCP
}

export function Logo({ className, size = "md", showText = false, inline = true }: LogoProps) {
  const textSizeClasses = {
    sm: "text-sm",
    md: "text-base",
    lg: "text-xl md:text-2xl",
    xl: "text-2xl md:text-3xl",
  };

  return (
    <div className={cn("flex items-center gap-2", className)}>
      {/* Use inline SVG by default for better FCP performance */}
      {inline ? (
        <LogoSvg size={size} />
      ) : (
        <img
          src="/logo.svg"
          alt="Tamiini"
          className={cn("flex-shrink-0 w-auto", 
            size === "sm" && "h-8 w-8",
            size === "md" && "h-12 w-12",
            size === "lg" && "h-16 w-16 md:h-20 md:w-20",
            size === "xl" && "h-24 w-24 md:h-32 md:w-32"
          )}
          width={size === "sm" ? 32 : size === "md" ? 48 : size === "lg" ? 80 : 128}
          height={size === "sm" ? 32 : size === "md" ? 48 : size === "lg" ? 80 : 128}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Optional Text */}
      {showText && (
        <span className={cn("font-bold text-foreground", textSizeClasses[size])}>
          Tamiini
        </span>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
  new_string: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface LogoProps {
  className?: string;
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl";
  showText?: boolean;
}

/** Uses the logo from public/logo.svg everywhere (nav, pages, etc.). */
export function Logo({ className, size = "md", showText = false }: LogoProps) {
  const textSizeClasses = {
    sm: "text-sm",
    md: "text-base",
    lg: "text-xl md:text-2xl",
    xl: "text-2xl md:text-3xl",
  };

  const sizeClasses = {
    sm: "h-6 w-auto",
    md: "h-8 w-auto",
    lg: "h-12 w-auto md:h-16 md:w-auto",
    xl: "h-16 w-auto md:h-20 md:w-auto",
  };

  return (
    <div className={cn("flex items-center gap-2", className)}>
      <img
        src="/logo.svg"
        alt="Logo"
        className={cn("flex-shrink-0", sizeClasses[size])}
        width={size === "sm" ? 96 : size === "md" ? 128 : size === "lg" ? 160 : 192}
        height={size === "sm" ? 28 : size === "md" ? 38 : size === "lg" ? 48 : 60}
      />
      {showText && (
        <span className={cn("font-bold text-foreground", textSizeClasses[size])}>
          Tamiini
        </span>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

<think>**Evaluating component structure**

I’m thinking about how the Separator is a client component. I wonder if our server page can still import and use it. Next.js allows server components to import client ones, but that might make the entire page a client. Actually, server components can import client ones, which means they become part of the client boundary. That’s fine, but I think I’ll avoid using Separator and keep it simple with just divs. Instead, I might use `Badge` and `Card`. So, I’ll create a local subcomponent called `ScreenshotMock` that uses lucide icons and fits within the server component’s requirements.
**Implementing ScreenshotMock component**

I’m planning to implement the `ScreenshotMock` function, which will take in a title, icon, and children as props. Inside, I'll create a layout that includes a rounded border and background. I want to arrange the title and icon using flex for better alignment, and I’ll add a `Badge` for the demo indication as well. 

For showcasing different `Badge` variants, like "Urgente" and "OK," I’ll create a sample UI for stock batches that lists items with their statuses on the right. I’ll remember to use the `outline` variant for `Badge` where applicable to fit my design.
**Implementing features array**

I’m looking to create a `features` array. It'll include objects with a title, description, and screenshot. I need to use the `t` function since `getTranslations("demo")` gives me access to nested translation keys. Next-intl supports dot notation, which means I can call `t("features.batchTitle")`. I should search for how nested keys are used in the code, maybe looking at instances like `t("toastUnexpected")` or `t("settings-general.title")`. I might also use `rg` for anything like `t("steps.` so I can inspect i18n usage effectively.</think>

+import { getTranslations, setRequestLocale } from "next-intl/server";
+import { Link } from "@/i18n/navigation";
+import { Logo } from "@/components/logo";
+import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
+import { Card } from "@/components/ui/card";
+import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge";
+import { ArrowRight, Mail, Package, Search, Sparkles, Timer } from "lucide-react";
+
+const DEMO_PIN = "002222";
+
+function ScreenshotMock({
+  variant,
+}: {
+  variant: "batches" | "tracking" | "search" | "suppliers";
+}) {
+  if (variant === "batches") {
+    return (
+      <div className="rounded-lg border bg-card p-3">
+        <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-3 mb-2">
+          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">

+          </div>
+          <Badge variant="secondary">Demo</Badge>
+        </div>

+          <div className="flex items-center justify-between text-sm">
+            <span className="truncate">Camarão</span>
+            <span className="text-muted-foreground">Expira em 2 dias</span>
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5">
+            <Badge variant="outline">Urgente</Badge>
+            <Badge variant="outline">12/03</Badge>

+        </div>

+  }

+          </div>

+            </div>

+export default async function DemoPage({
+  params,

+  variant: "batches" | "tracking" | "search" | "suppliers";
+}) {

+import { NextIntlClientProvider } from "next-intl";
+import { getMessages, setRequestLocale } from "next-intl/server";
+import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
+import { routing } from "@/i18n/routing";
+import { CSPostHogProvider } from "@/components/posthog-provider";
+import { Toaster } from "@/components/ui/sonner";
+import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
+
+export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
+
+export default async function DemoLocaleLayout({
+  children,
+  params,
+}: {
+  children: React.ReactNode;
+  params: Promise<{ locale: string }>;
+}) {
+  const { locale } = await params;
+
+  if (!routing.locales.includes(locale as "pt-PT" | "en")) {
+    notFound();
+  }
+
+  setRequestLocale(locale);
+  const messages = await getMessages();
+
+  return (
+    <CSPostHogProvider>
+      <NextIntlClientProvider messages={messages} locale={locale}>
+        <div className={cn("relative flex min-h-screen flex-col")}>
+          <main className="flex-1 w-full px-4 md:px-6 py-6 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
+            {children}
+          </main>
+          <Toaster position="top-center" richColors />
+        </div>
+      </NextIntlClientProvider>
+    </CSPostHogProvider>
+  );
+}
+

4. For "data reset every time" and "predefined dataset" - options:
   - **Option A**: When user clicks "Ver demonstração", set a cookie/session flag `demo=true`. In the app, when `demo=true`, we don't use real DB - we serve a static JSON or in-memory seed data. All "writes" go to a client-side store (e.g. React state/localStorage that we clear on next demo load). Complex.
   - **Option B**: Use the existing restaurant B (PIN 002222) as "demo" but:
     - On demo entry: run a server action that resets that restaurant's data to a seed (e.g. delete all batches and re-insert seed batches). Then log in as that restaurant and set a cookie `demo=1`. In the app, when demo=1, we never persist writes (all mutations no-op or redirect to read-only). That would require every mutation to check "if demo then don't persist". Messy.
   - **Option C**: Create a separate "demo" tenant in the DB that has a known ID. On every demo access, run a job that resets that tenant's data to seed (truncate + insert). Users log in as that tenant. We need to ensure only this tenant gets reset and no one uses it for real. And we'd need to either prevent persistence when in demo mode (so multiple concurrent users don't overwrite each other) or accept that demo data gets overwritten on each new demo visit - which would mean "reset on every access" = when a new person clicks "Ver demonstração", we reset the demo tenant and they get fresh seed data; but if two people use demo at once they'd share the same data. The requirement says "reset every time it is accessed" - so each time someone enters demo we reset. So Option C: one demo tenant, on "Enter demo" we reset its data to seed and log them in. Concurrent users would see the same seed data and any changes could be overwritten by the next reset - so we might want to make demo read-only (no persist) to avoid confusion. Actually re-reading: "Do NOT persist any changes" - so we must not save any changes in demo mode. So we need either:
     - Demo mode uses real DB but we never write (all create/update/delete are no-ops when session is demo). Reads would go to the demo tenant which we reset on each demo entry. So: on "Ver demonstração" → reset demo tenant data → log in as demo tenant → set demo flag in session → in all mutations check demo flag and skip if set.
     - Or: demo mode uses in-memory/static data only, no DB. So we'd need to change the app to support a "data source" that could be either API (real DB) or static. Bigger refactor.

   1. Reset that tenant's data to a fixed seed (server action).
   2. Log the user in as that tenant (set session).

/**
 * Set demo mode cookie (readable by client for UI label and tour)
 * Call after creating session for demo restaurant
 */
export async function setDemoCookie(): Promise<void> {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + SESSION_DURATION_MS);
  cookieStore.set(DEMO_COOKIE_NAME, "1", {
    httpOnly: false, // So client can show "Modo demonstração" and trigger tour
    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
    sameSite: "lax",
    path: "/",
    expires: expiresAt,
  });
}

export async function clearDemoCookie(): Promise<void> {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  cookieStore.delete(DEMO_COOKIE_NAME);
}

/**
 * Returns true if the current request has the demo cookie set (server-side check for no-op mutations)
 */
export async function getIsDemoSession(): Promise<boolean> {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  const demoCookie = cookieStore.get(DEMO_COOKIE_NAME);
  return demoCookie?.value === "1";
}
